Title: Benzylisoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis in opium poppy: an update

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutical opiates and related medicinal alkaloids
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates uptake of multiple BIAs and improves opiate production in engineered yeast; likely contributes to intercellular alkaloid transport in opium poppy latex.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BUP1 is a benzylisoquinoline alkaloid uptake permease discovered from opium poppy sequences linked to BIA gene clusters. 2. In engineered yeast, BUP1 increased uptake and intracellular levels of several BIAs including norcoclaurine, reticuline, thebaine, codeine and morphine, and improved opiate titres from precursors/intermediates. 3. In opium poppy, BUP1 is predominantly expressed in latex and localized to the plasma membrane, supporting a transport role in alkaloid partitioning.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: purine permease-type transporter; benzylisoquinoline alkaloid uptake permease
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; uptake assays; metabolite profiling; subcellular localization; expression analysis; VIGS of BUP transcripts
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BUP1 was the only active BUP gene linked to the noscapine biosynthetic cluster; silencing did not strongly alter plant alkaloid levels, likely due to redundancy.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for microbial pathway engineering to improve uptake/retention of BIA intermediates and end products; may be stacked with biosynthetic genes in modular cocultures.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a BIA uptake permease with substrate-selective transport activity; likely contributes redundantly to alkaloid transport.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BUP4 was among six active BUP isoforms functionally characterized. 2. It showed a distinctive substrate acceptance profile in yeast. 3. It was predominantly expressed in latex in opium poppy.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: purine permease-type transporter; benzylisoquinoline alkaloid uptake permease
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely functionally redundant with other BUP transporters.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BIA uptake permease isoform with probable tissue-specific transport role.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BUP5 was functionally characterized as an active BUP transporter in yeast. 2. It showed a distinctive BIA substrate profile. 3. In opium poppy, BUP5 was mostly expressed in roots.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: purine permease-type transporter; benzylisoquinoline alkaloid uptake permease
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root for some BIAs such as sanguinarine/magnoflorine

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BIA uptake permease isoform likely contributing to alkaloid transport in latex.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BUP6 was one of six active BUP transporters. 2. It displayed isoform-specific substrate uptake in yeast. 3. It was predominantly expressed in latex.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: purine permease-type transporter; benzylisoquinoline alkaloid uptake permease

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active BIA transporter isoform with low stem expression.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BUP2 was functionally active in yeast transport assays. 2. It displayed substrate selectivity distinct from other BUP isoforms. 3. In opium poppy, BUP2 showed low expression in stem.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: purine permease-type transporter; benzylisoquinoline alkaloid uptake permease

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active BIA transporter isoform in yeast, but transcript not detected in opium poppy tissues examined.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BUP3 was among six active BUP transporter isoforms identified functionally in yeast. 2. Its transport profile differed from other BUPs. 3. BUP3 transcripts were not detected in analyzed opium poppy tissues.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: purine permease-type transporter; benzylisoquinoline alkaloid uptake permease

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suggested positive regulator of early BIA biosynthesis through activation of TYDC promoter.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A wound-induced WRKY highly expressed in capsules binds W-box elements in promoters of BIA biosynthetic genes. 2. Yeast one-hybrid confirmed binding to and activation of a TYDC promoter. 3. The evidence supports a regulatory role in early BIA biosynthesis, but downstream physiological metabolite effects were not fully characterized.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro DNA-binding assay; yeast one-hybrid
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in capsules; wound induced
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific gene symbol not provided in the review.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hypothesized contributor to coordinated regulation of noscapine pathway genes based on promoter motif occurrence and expression pattern.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A MYB gene preferentially expressed in leaves was isolated from opium poppy. 2. MYB motifs occur in promoters of genes in the noscapine cluster. 3. Direct functional validation for metabolic regulation was not reported in this review.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; promoter motif analysis; expression profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as a candidate regulator rather than a fully validated core regulator.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Correlationally associated with thebaine and oripavine accumulation among cultivars.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Expression correlation across 10 cultivars suggested possible regulatory involvement of a CH3 gene in BIA metabolism. 2. Association was strongest with thebaine and oripavine accumulation. 3. Functional characterization was lacking.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Insufficiently characterized; exact molecular function unclear.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates L-tyrosine to tyramine, contributing to dopamine formation upstream of BIA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: pyridoxal phosphate-dependent aromatic amino acid decarboxylase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides entry of primary metabolism into BIA pathway by generating tyramine/dopamine precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TYDC catalyzes an early precursor step from L-tyrosine. 2. TYDC genes were isolated and enzymes functionally characterized in opium poppy. 3. TYDC is implicated in the initial stage of BIA biosynthesis and is a transcriptional target of a WRKY regulator.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; enzyme characterization; promoter activation evidence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transcript localized mainly in companion cells
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: latex; roots depending on BIA chemotype

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transaminates L-tyrosine to 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate, contributing to 4-HPAA production upstream of BIA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies 4-HPAA precursor branch for (S)-norcoclaurine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TyrAT genes were isolated and functionally characterized in opium poppy. 2. The enzyme supports generation of 4-HPAA precursor. 3. Considerable biochemical and physiological support places TyrAT in the initial stage of BIA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; enzyme characterization

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: opium poppy; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Papaver somniferum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the Pictet-Spengler condensation of dopamine and 4-HPAA to form exclusively (S)-norcoclaurine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms the first committed intermediate of BIA biosynthesis; improved NCS variants enhance microbial BIA production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCS produces (S)-norcoclaurine, the first committed BIA intermediate. 2. Three active opium poppy isoforms have been characterized, including fusion-type PsNCS3 with four tandem active domains. 3. Tandem fusions may improve the intrinsically low catalytic efficiency and are relevant for metabolic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme characterization; structural analysis of orthologs; heterologous pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: enzyme detected in sieve elements; transcripts mainly in companion cells
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: committed-step enzyme; low-efficiency bottleneck in microbial engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PsNCS1, PsNCS2 and PsNCS3 described; NCS tandem fusions appear restricted to Papaveraceae.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: opium poppy; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Papaver somniferum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates (S)-norcoclaurine at C6 en route to (S)-reticuline.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for conversion of norcoclaurine toward reticuline and downstream alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 6OMT catalyzes an early O-methylation in the common BIA pathway. 2. Structural work on orthologs clarified catalytic residues likely conserved in opium poppy. 3. 6OMT can participate in a functional heterodimer with OMT2 in noscapine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme characterization; ortholog structural studies; heterologous reconstruction; heterodimer assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: enzyme in sieve elements; transcripts mainly in companion cells

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: opium poppy; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Papaver somniferum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: N-methylates coclaurine to form N-methylcoclaurine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to common pathway to reticuline; silencing increases papaverine, supporting NH pathway as main papaverine route.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CNMT is a common-pathway enzyme forming N-methylcoclaurine. 2. Structural work on an ortholog clarified likely conserved catalytic mechanism. 3. VIGS suppression increased papaverine accumulation, arguing against the N-methylated route as the main papaverine pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme characterization; VIGS; structural inference from ortholog
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: enzyme in sieve elements; transcripts mainly in companion cells

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates N-methylcoclaurine at the 3' position en route to reticuline.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports reticuline formation and downstream BIA accumulation; methyl jasmonate induces expression; overexpression increases total latex alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NMCH catalyzes a late common-pathway hydroxylation before reticuline formation. 2. Its expression is induced by methyl jasmonate in cell cultures and was linked to sanguinarine-associated responses. 3. Overexpression increased total latex alkaloid content with limited profile changes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme characterization; transcriptional suppression evidence; overexpression; hormone response analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: enzyme in sieve elements; transcripts mainly in companion cells

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: opium poppy; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Papaver somniferum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates 3'-hydroxy-N-methylcoclaurine at the 4' position to form (S)-reticuline.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: (S)-reticuline; papaverine; noscapine; morphinans and other BIAs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for reticuline formation and broadly affects all major BIAs; OMT2/6OMT heterodimer versions also participate in noscapine branch 4'-O-methylation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4'OMT generates the central branch intermediate (S)-reticuline. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of 4'OMT2 reduced multiple major BIAs in leaves. 3. 4'OMT activity also appears in heterodimeric form in the noscapine pathway with OMT2:OMT3 or OMT2:6OMT.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme characterization; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; heterologous assays; VIGS-related pathway studies
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: enzyme in sieve elements; transcripts mainly in companion cells

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes (S)-reticuline to 1,2-dehydroreticuline with the P450 domain and reduces it to (R)-reticuline with the aldo-keto reductase domain.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential gateway enzyme for morphinan biosynthesis; silencing reduces morphine, codeine and thebaine while causing (S)-reticuline/noscapine accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. REPI/STORR catalyzes the stereochemical inversion from (S)- to (R)-reticuline. 2. It is a unique fusion of cytochrome P450 and aldo-keto reductase domains. 3. Silencing demonstrated its key physiological role at the entry to morphinan biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene silencing; biochemical characterization; heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: silencing diverts flux to non-morphinan BIAs such as noscapine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also called STORR; proposed advantages include coexpression, colocalization and substrate channeling.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: opium poppy; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Papaver somniferum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes stereo- and regiospecific phenol coupling of (R)-reticuline to salutaridine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates conversion of (R)-reticuline into pentacyclic morphinan branch intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SalSyn/CYP719B1 converts (R)-reticuline to salutaridine. 2. It is part of the thebaine biosynthetic gene cluster. 3. Expression is induced by methyl jasmonate in capsules together with increased thebaine accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; heterologous reconstruction; hormone-response studies
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: early specialized-pathway enzyme in sieve elements
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate induces expression in capsules

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: opium poppy; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Papaver somniferum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces salutaridine to (7S)-salutaridinol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for progression from salutaridine to thebaine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SalR converts salutaridine to salutaridinol. 2. It participates in clustered thebaine biosynthetic steps. 3. It is induced by methyl jasmonate in capsules and contributes to increased thebaine accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; heterologous reconstruction; expression studies
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: detected in sieve elements and laticifers
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate induces expression in capsules

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: opium poppy; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Papaver somniferum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates salutaridinol at the 7-O position to generate salutaridinol 7-O-acetate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies direct precursor for thebaine formation; overexpression accumulates morphinan alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SalAT produces salutaridinol 7-O-acetate, the immediate precursor of thebaine. 2. It resides in the thebaine gene cluster. 3. Overexpression in opium poppy increased morphinan accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; heterologous reconstruction; transgenic overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: detected in sieve elements and laticifers

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of salutaridinol 7-O-acetate to thebaine, replacing a previously presumed spontaneous step.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms thebaine; inclusion in engineered yeast markedly increases thebaine production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THS was discovered as the missing enzyme for thebaine formation. 2. It is a dimeric PR10 protein and is located in the thebaine gene cluster. 3. Functional validation in yeast and transcript suppression in poppy confirmed its in vivo role.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous yeast expression; transcript suppression/VIGS; biochemical characterization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly/solely in laticifers
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review text abbreviates as THS in main sections; later one sentence says THB, evidently same enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: major pathway from thebaine to morphine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes O-demethylation of thebaine at C6 to neopinone and also O-demethylates oripavine to neomorphinone in the minor pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drives major route from thebaine toward codeine/morphine; enzyme promiscuity affects multiple alkaloid substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T6ODM catalyzes key O-demethylation steps downstream of thebaine. 2. Crystal structure revealed a large binding cavity consistent with broad substrate promiscuity. 3. Tandem gene copies exist in the opium poppy genome and the enzyme is important in microbial opioid engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; crystal structure; heterologous reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly/solely in laticifers
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key downstream control point in opioid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Five tandem copies plus partial/pseudogene variants reported.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: minor pathway from thebaine and terminal conversion of codeine to morphine
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: morphine; oripavine and minor products depending on substrate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes O-demethylation of codeine to morphine and also acts on thebaine and related morphinan intermediates in minor reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Main terminal enzyme producing morphine from codeine; also contributes to minor branch reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CODM is a major morphine-forming enzyme preferring codeine as substrate. 2. It was among the first 2-ODDs shown to catalyze O-demethylation. 3. Multiple tandem gene copies are present and the enzyme contributes to several minor reactions besides codeine demethylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; heterologous reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly/solely in laticifers

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bidirectionally isomerizes neopinone to codeinone and neomorphinone to morphinone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Prevents carbon loss to neopine/neomorphine side products and promotes efficient morphine/codeine formation; silencing reduces morphine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NISO was discovered as the missing enzyme for a previously presumed spontaneous isomerization. 2. It is highly expressed in latex and VIGS significantly reduced morphine levels. 3. Inclusion of NISO in yeast improves thebaine-to-codeine/morphine conversion by limiting side-product formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: biochemical characterization; site-directed mutagenesis; VIGS; heterologous yeast expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly abundant in latex; predominantly/solely in laticifers
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point preventing side-route carbon loss

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces codeinone to codeine and morphinone to morphine; also irreversibly reduces neopinone and neomorphinone to side products neopine and neomorphine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for codeine and morphine formation but can also divert flux to unwanted side products in heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COR catalyzes reversible reductions from codeinone/morphinone to codeine/morphine. 2. It also produces neopine/neomorphine from neopinone/neomorphinone, though these do not accumulate in planta because of NISO. 3. RNAi suppression decreased morphinans and historically caused unexpected reticuline accumulation due to off-target effects on REPI reductase domain.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme characterization; RNAi; site-directed mutagenesis; heterologous pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly/solely in laticifers
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can generate unwanted by-products neopine and neomorphine in engineered yeast.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: opium poppy; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Papaver somniferum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C–C phenol coupling of (S)-reticuline to form (S)-scoulerine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Creates the common branch intermediate scoulerine for both sanguinarine and noscapine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two BBE genes were isolated and functionally characterized from opium poppy. 2. Isoforms are differentially expressed in stem and roots, suggesting differential contribution to noscapine versus sanguinarine biosynthesis. 3. BBE lies outside the noscapine cluster, likely because of its dual role in root and aerial pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; enzyme characterization; expression profiling; heterologous reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: early specialized-pathway enzyme in sieve elements; differential stem/root expression

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: opium poppy; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Papaver somniferum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Forms a methylenedioxy bridge on scoulerine to yield cheilanthifoline.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for sanguinarine branch and expressed consistently with root-specific accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CFS catalyzes the first methylenedioxy bridge formation from scoulerine. 2. It is exclusively or predominantly expressed in roots. 3. Root expression supports organ-specific sanguinarine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; expression profiling; heterologous reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: exclusively or predominantly expressed in roots

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: opium poppy; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Papaver somniferum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Pichia pastoris
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Forms a second methylenedioxy bridge to convert cheilanthifoline to stylopine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to root-specific sanguinarine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPS catalyzes cheilanthifoline-to-stylopine conversion. 2. It is exclusively or predominantly expressed in roots. 3. Expression pattern matches root-specific sanguinarine accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; expression profiling; heterologous reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: exclusively or predominantly expressed in roots

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: N-methylates cis-protoberberine substrates such as stylopine and canadine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promiscuous shared enzyme connecting protoberberine intermediates to both sanguinarine and noscapine branches.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TNMT accepts stylopine and canadine, matching its dual role in sanguinarine and noscapine biosynthesis. 2. Structural work on an ortholog explained cis-substrate specificity. 3. The gene is excluded from the noscapine cluster likely because it is shared with root-specific sanguinarine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme characterization; ortholog structural analysis; heterologous reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates N-methylstylopine at C14, causing ring opening to yield protopine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Root-specific enzyme required for protopine formation in sanguinarine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MSH catalyzes ring-opening hydroxylation of N-methylstylopine to protopine. 2. It is root-specific. 3. It functions downstream of TNMT in the sanguinarine branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; expression profiling; heterologous reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root specific

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates protopine leading to spontaneous ring rearrangement and formation of dihydrosanguinarine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces the immediate precursor of sanguinarine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P6H converts protopine toward dihydrosanguinarine. 2. It acts after MSH in the root-specific sanguinarine branch. 3. It has been functionally characterized in the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; heterologous reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: opium poppy; Saccharomyces cerevisiae ex vivo
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes two-electron oxidation/aromatization to form sanguinarine from dihydrosanguinarine and papaverine from tetrahydropapaverine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Terminal oxidase shared by sanguinarine and papaverine pathways; higher activity toward dihydrosanguinarine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DBOX forms sanguinarine by oxidizing dihydrosanguinarine. 2. It also catalyzes the last step of papaverine biosynthesis from tetrahydropapaverine. 3. The enzyme is root-expressed and exhibits higher turnover for dihydrosanguinarine than for tetrahydropapaverine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro and in vivo functional characterization; heterologous/ex vivo assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: exclusively expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Root-restricted expression raises questions about papaverine biosynthesis sites versus accumulation sites.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the reverse of DBOX with exclusive activity toward sanguinarine, reducing it as a likely detoxification mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: May reduce cytotoxic effects of sanguinarine in the plant.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Several SanR isoforms were isolated and characterized. 2. They show exclusive activity toward sanguinarine. 3. The reaction has been suggested to protect the plant from sanguinarine cytotoxicity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: isoform isolation and characterization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in alkaloid homeostasis rather than forward biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates scoulerine at C9 in the noscapine pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required early step in noscapine biosynthesis; SOMT1 silencing also affects papaverine because of residual side activities.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SOMT catalyzes an early methylation in the noscapine branch. 2. VIGS and biochemical characterization supported its pathway role. 3. SOMT1 also shows residual 3'- and 7-O-methyltransferase activity affecting papaverine metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; biochemical characterization; heterologous reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Closes the isoquinoline moiety by methylenedioxy bridge formation to form canadine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential early tailoring step in noscapine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAS catalyzes methylenedioxy bridge formation to canadine. 2. It lies within the noscapine cluster. 3. Its function has been supported by VIGS and biochemical studies.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; biochemical characterization; heterologous reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates N-methylcanadine at position 1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates a series of oxidative steps unique to noscapine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82Y1 performs the first of consecutive hydroxylations after N-methylcanadine formation. 2. It resides in the noscapine cluster. 3. Its function was defined by biochemical characterization and pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; biochemical characterization; heterologous reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates 1-hydroxy-N-methylcanadine at the 13-O position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies substrate for protective acetylation in noscapine pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82X2 is the second oxidative enzyme in the late noscapine branch. 2. It is encoded in the noscapine cluster. 3. Functional characterization supported its sequential role upstream of AT1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; biochemical characterization; heterologous reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds a protective acetyl group to 1,13-dihydroxy-N-methylcanadine prior to further hydroxylation and ring opening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables progression through late oxidative steps in noscapine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AT1 acetylates a late noscapine intermediate at the 13-O position. 2. The acetyl group is proposed to protect the intermediate before CYP82X1 action. 3. It belongs to the noscapine gene cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; biochemical characterization; heterologous reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the acetylated N-methylcanadine intermediate at C8, followed by spontaneous ring opening and N7-C8 bridge cleavage to form 4'-O-desmethyl-3-acetylpapaveroxine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates the direct substrate for heterodimeric 4'-O-methylation in noscapine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82X1 performs the last oxidative step before heterodimeric methylation. 2. Its action leads to spontaneous ring opening. 3. It is encoded in the noscapine cluster and validated biochemically.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; biochemical characterization; heterologous reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: A heterodimeric O-methyltransferase catalyzing 4'-O-methylation of 4'-O-desmethyl-3-acetylpapaveroxine to form 3-O-acetylpapaveroxine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Performs a unique late methylation essential for noscapine formation; OMT2 is the key dimerization subunit.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OMT2 alone was implicated by VIGS but showed no expected activity until coexpressed with OMT3 or 6OMT. 2. Purified heterodimers established 4'-O-desmethyl-3-acetylpapaveroxine as the true substrate. 3. Both OMT2:OMT3 and OMT2:6OMT are physiologically relevant, with OMT2 crucial for dimerization and activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; yeast co-expression; in vitro assays with recombinant heterodimers; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This was the first demonstration that plant O-methyltransferases can form functional heterodimers.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes the ester of 3-O-acetylpapaveroxine prior to final noscapine-forming oxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary penultimate step in noscapine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CXE1 hydrolyzes 3-O-acetylpapaveroxine. 2. It functions near the end of the noscapine pathway. 3. It has been validated in opium poppy and heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; heterologous reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly/solely in laticifers

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes dehydrogenation of narcotine hemiacetal to produce noscapine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms noscapine as the terminal product of the branch.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NOS catalyzes the final dehydrogenation to noscapine. 2. It is among the late genes in the noscapine cluster. 3. Together with CXE1, it completes the terminal two steps in the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization; heterologous reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly/solely in laticifers

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C8-C2' coupling of reticuline to produce corytuberine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defines one proposed route to magnoflorine via corytuberine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C. japonica CTS is functionally characterized and provides the model for magnoflorine biosynthesis. 2. An opium poppy ortholog had not yet been isolated in this review. 3. Lack of tembetarine acceptance by CjCTS supports corytuberine-first route in opium poppy.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ortholog biochemical characterization; pathway inference
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species and applied species differ; gene not yet isolated from opium poppy at time of review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: N-methylates corytuberine to form magnoflorine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes magnoflorine accumulation in roots, supporting corytuberine as the major route intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNMT was isolated from opium poppy roots and comprehensively characterized. 2. It accepts corytuberine as substrate. 3. VIGS suppression reduced magnoflorine and increased corytuberine without affecting reticuline or tembetarine, supporting the corytuberine route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; biochemical characterization; VIGS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: preferentially expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Magnoflorine stereochemistry in opium poppy remains unresolved.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates reticuline at the 7-O position to form laudanine in the proposed N-methylated papaverine route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of a proposed alternative route to papaverine, but suppression did not affect papaverine levels.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 7OMT was proposed to function in the N-methylated papaverine route. 2. VIGS suppression did not alter papaverine levels. 3. Comparative transcriptomics showed down-regulation in a high-papaverine cultivar, arguing against this route as dominant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; comparative transcriptomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence weighs against major contribution to papaverine biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates norreticuline at the 7-O position in the N-desmethyl papaverine pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly supports the NH route as the main papaverine pathway; silencing decreases papaverine accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. N7OMT is specific to the NH papaverine route. 2. VIGS silencing significantly decreased papaverine accumulation. 3. N7OMT was up-regulated in a high-papaverine cultivar and proposed intermediates accumulated in latex, supporting the NH pathway as the major route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; metabolite detection; comparative transcriptomics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key discriminating step for papaverine branch choice

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 7-O-demethylation of papaverine to pacodine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds new information on papaverine metabolism beyond biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P7ODM was isolated as a novel papaverine-metabolizing enzyme. 2. It converts papaverine to pacodine by 7-O-demethylation. 3. The finding expands knowledge of papaverine metabolism though not core forward biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation and characterization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents downstream papaverine metabolism rather than papaverine biosynthetic assembly.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TYDC promoter; likely additional BIA biosynthetic gene promoters containing W-box motifs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates TYDC and likely other early pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-responsive positive regulator of early precursor entry into BIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suggests enhanced early BIA biosynthetic capacity through transcriptional activation of TYDC.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A wound-induced WRKY binds W-box motifs in BIA biosynthetic gene promoters. 2. Yeast one-hybrid confirmed binding to and activation of the TYDC promoter. 3. It is highly expressed in capsules and is implicated as a positive regulator of early alkaloid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA-binding assay; yeast one-hybrid; expression profiling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in capsules; wound induced
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific locus/gene symbol not given in the review.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate regulator identified from thebaine-accumulating mutants
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with altered thebaine accumulation in T-DNA insertion mutants.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A WRKY gene was first identified through analysis of thebaine-accumulating T-DNA insertion mutants. 2. This linked WRKY family members to BIA regulation in opium poppy. 3. Direct target genes and mechanism were not established in this review.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific gene identity and direct function remain unclear.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: genes in the noscapine biosynthetic cluster containing MYB motifs
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate coordinator of noscapine-cluster gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hypothesized to contribute to coordinated noscapine-pathway regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A MYB gene preferentially expressed in leaves was isolated from opium poppy. 2. MYB motifs occur in promoters of all 10 genes in the noscapine cluster. 3. Direct functional validation as a regulator of noscapine biosynthesis was not yet available.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; promoter motif analysis; expression profiling

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: cultivar-correlated candidate associated with thebaine/oripavine levels
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression correlates with thebaine and oripavine accumulation among cultivars.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Correlation analysis across 10 cultivars implicated a CH3 gene in BIA regulation. 2. Association was strongest with thebaine and oripavine accumulation. 3. Functional mechanism was not characterized.
